feed.title: SAP Community - SD (Sales and Distribution)
feed.subtitle: SD (Sales and Distribution) Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: I create sales order and I do delivery when I press post goods issue this message sent to me
Sales BOM: Is it possible to have Sales BOM that could consist of Product and services?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Gurus,

Regarding Sales BOM, Is it possible to have Sales BOM that could consist of Product and services in standard SAP? 

Thanks

entry.title: Sales BOM: Can we have components in the Sales BOM from different plants?
entry.content:
Sales BOM: Can we have components in the Sales BOM from different plants?

For example: Sales BOM Header from Plant A, and sub-items from Plant B & Plant C.

entry.title: Manage Sales Order App - is it possible to restrict drop down list for button "Reject all"?
entry.content:
Hi,
I' ve noticed that in FIORI app ID F1873 - Manage Sales Order, when using button "Reject all" there will open pop up window in order to choose rejection reason - all which were set in the system. Is it possible to restrict this drop down list similarly to solution which already works for FIORI VA01/VA02 described in SAP note 2477027?
link -> 2477027 - Restriction of values for order reason and rejection reason in dropdown lists 

I know that in this note there is short enigmatic sentence "Restrictions: This function is supported differently, only partially, or not at all in SAP Fiori apps." Never the less it is not precisely defined if it can't be done for drop down I've mentioned. Maybe someone faced such a problem? Do you think that only solution is to raised such development idea in SAP Customer Influence site?

Drop down list which i want to restrict:

entry.title: Product Availability Check: storage location
entry.content:
Hello,

We have the scenario regarding the MRP Area in our industry:

Outside the area, we have three storage locations: the Storage location with supplies for production (ROH), the Storage location for the production of HALB and FERT, and the Storage location for sales, where we have imported goods (HAWA) and FERT. We configured it this way so that these warehouses have visibility of all the requirements and can generate planned orders and requisitions correctly. Inside the area, there are the Storage locations that are not used for sales (warranty, discard...).

The issue is that some HAWA items we sell are also used in production as raw material for FERT, and the balance of this HAWA in production sometimes doesn’t have a production order attached and ends up being sold. Is there a way to restrict the ATP and sales view to the sales warehouse only? Or is there a better suggestion for a solution?

entry.title: Quantity is not updating in Contract/outline agreement when we create SO with reference to Billing.
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Due to quantity descripency we need to create a sales order with reference to Billing where qty should be update in contract as well.

Process flow:

Contract/outline Agreement: 1000 qty

Sales Order: 100 qty

Delivery: 100 qty

Billing 100 qty

Order with reference to above billing: 20 qty

The actual qty was delivered to customer less than sales order qty. 

Actual Qty Delivered: 80 qty

Sales Order created with: 100 qty,      Delivery : 100 qty,      Billing: 100 qty 

Second order created with reference to Billing for remaining 20 qty.

Once customer received the the qty and invoice he got only 80 qty instead of 100 qty in Billing. now we need to deliver 20 more qty to customer. for the tracking purpose we are creating our sales order with reference to billing/invoice which was sent to customer, we are changing qty in order with remaining 20 qty and processing further .. now problem is that 20 qty are not reflecting in our contract/outline agreement.

After creating first SO with 100 qty  remaining quantity in contract/agreement  was 900, and after creating second order with 20 qty  (created with reference to Billing) I am expecting 20 qty will also reduce and remaining qty 880 will be in contract/agreemet BUT contract/agreemet is still with 900 qty and there is no changes in qty in contract/agreement after creating sales order with Billing.

I want to achieve it when sales order is created with reference to Billing, 20 qty should update contract/Agreement.

Thanks

entry.title: VB21 and VK11 with same condition and the tables in same order but does not show same in both transa
entry.content: VB21 same contion typeVK11 same condition type

entry.title: Setup Approval Process for Sales Order in S4Hana Cloud
entry.content:
Hi community and all experts,

I would like to seek some help in setting up the approval process for Sales Order with a Net Value Condition. I've tried but the approval process doesn't kick in when the order reached or exceed that limit.

This is what I've setup so far in the system.

1st, i define the approval request in the configurations.

2nd, i setup the workflow for sales order with a net amount condition.

3rd, i created a custom logic for the workflow.

I've also checked to make sure the workflow scenario is activated.

However, my test Sales Order didn't trigger the approval process that I've setup.

May i ask what have i missed or could it be my setup is wrong?

Thank you very much for all guidance.

Best Regards,

Dan

entry.title: Internal Pricing Error after using BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am creating a goods movement whenever changes in the SD meet certain conditions using BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE in user exit MV45AFZZ - USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE.

I am not passing any price or pricing conditions to BAPI. However, I am getting an popup message STOP - Internal Pricing Error.

This could be because the PRICING_REFRESH function module has been called up in the BAPI, even though you did not require it.

Please provide your valuable inputs.

Best Regards,

Nicole

entry.title: Is it possible to post fixed tax amount in countries with tax jurisdiction (e.g. US)?
entry.content:
We have an unusual request to post sales tax as fixed amount in the sales orders. Naturally, that also needs to flow into the billing document and post to accounting.

In this scenario, the sales are done in a third party system and the goal is to post the price and tax as fixed amount, exactly as that system calculated. (The issues of proper tax reporting and audit are outside of the scope of this question.)

We were able to make this work in countries where tax jurisdiction is not used. (Added new condition with Tax type and Amount instead of %.) However, if we use the same configuration for US or Canada, we end up with error FF805 "Tax statement is missing...". Via debugger and note search (e.g. this one), we concluded that tax jurisdiction conditions (JRn) must always be present for the countries with tax jurisdiction.

The attempt to simply add JR1 condition on top of the custom "flat tax amount" one did not help. The error happens in FM FI_TAX_SV_BSEG_BSET_GROSS because in T_BSET the field TXJDP is blank. (All other fields, including TXJCD are filled in correctly.) Brute-forcing TXJCD value into TXJDP in debugger resulted in a similar error down the road ("SYST: Tax jurisdiction code at lowest level not transferred").

There are several questions about posting the fixed tax amount but they're not about US/Canada and I've not found any evidence it is feasible. At this point I'm looking at least for information that would confirm is it's possible at all or just flat out not possible.

To reiterate, this is specifically about countries with tax jurisdiction (such as US/Canada), we have a solution for other countries.

entry.title: SAD SD : Query of GST rate having differ for different unit price of material
entry.content: For materials with a unit price greater than Rs 1000, the GST rate should be 12%. However, for the same material, if the unit price is less than Rs 1000, the GST rate should be 5%. The HSN code remains the same for both cases. How can I configure this requirement in SAP

entry.title: Allocate products to Distribution channels by percentage not Quantity
entry.content:
Hello Dears, 
My client has a Pain point that when products are produced, representative comes first has edge to load quantity he wants with no considerations for another Reps . He needs to constrains this by SAP System but not by quantity he need it by percentage. He does not want product allocations as follows if for example he produces 1000 pcs daily 
Dist.Channel 1 : 500 PCS
Dist.Channel 2 : 300 PCS 
Dist.channel 3 : 200 PCS 

Production is not a fixed quantity daily , so he needs it to be for example: 
Dist.channel 1 : 50 % 
Dist.channel 2 : 30 % 
Dist.channel 3 : 20 % 

so does anything in SAP Standards fit this requirement ?

@

entry.title: Packing Issue due to missed stock posting in Inspection lot and QMHU table
entry.content:
Hi All,

Our client is setup HU managed warehouse (not EWM). Inspection lots are getting created at HU level. We are facing a weird issue on random basis.

At the time of Goods Receipt from external suppliers, Inspection lot gets generated at HU level and postings (321/311) happened on UD. QMHU table also updated properly.

But while doing goods receival process for STO, Inspection lot gets generated at HU level, but postings (321/311) does not happen on UD completion. And QMHU table is updating without stock posting (field: QMGBUCHHUM). When we try to use the same (batch) stock for goods issue with packing, system does not allow to complete packing and throws a dump  ( DBSQL_DUPLICATE_KEY_ERROR ) where system tries to insert the duplicate entry into QMHU for specific inspection lot and new packing handling unit.

To sort this issue temporarily, we try to debug the dump and try to remove the duplicates from QMHU. which further allows to pack for goods issue.

If anyone has experienced the same issue and have the detailed information about root cause and resolution. Please share the same.

Thank you in advance !

entry.title: The invoice amount field in the revenue schedule not automatically reflecting the invoice values
entry.content:
The invoice amount field in the revenue schedule in Manage Revenue accounting application should automatically reflect the invoice values after the creation of the billing document, but this is not happening. 

In our scenario, after billing, the invoice is not reflecting in the invoice amount field in the revenue schedule, But If we manually process  Z_FARRIC_REPOST_ADVANCED from SE38 for both sales order and its related invoices, then we run the FARR_RAI_MON for both sales orders and invoices. The invoice amount column will update in the revenue schedule in the revenue accounting app.

We need to update the invoice amount field value automatically without running this manual process.

How to solve this issue?

entry.title: Subscriptions: Sales Order without Customer
entry.content:
Scenario: We sell products that include the option to activate a subscription license after the sale. There is no additional price for the subscription, it is "free" to the customer.

Requirement: From an accounting perspective, we will assign a revenue amount to the subscription. But, we are thinking we will need two sales orders: (1) for the original sale to the customer and (2) a second order for the activation of the license.  The second order will not be able to have the customer as there is no further charge; but, we think we need it at the time of activation to create the RAR item.

Can we have a Sales Order without a customer?  Is there some other option we should be considering?

SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting

entry.title: Block sales order lines
entry.content:
Dear all,

We have a request from our customer. He would like the line(s) of the sales order to be blocked when the quantity is not full.
At the moment it doesn't block, so we need to go back to the orders to check and correct them if necessary.

Thanks for your help,

entry.title: Profit Center in sales order is not saved
entry.content:
Good morning,

In the material master, I have set the profit center. However, when I create a sales order with this material, the profit center is not filled.

In the sales order I get the message that the sales order is not complete and that the profit center is missing.

I can enter the profit center under Account Assignment on item level. I get no further error messages and then I get the message that the document is complete:

However, when I save it, the entry in profit center is deleted again, and I get the message that the document is incomplete:

What is happening here? Why can't I save the profit center in the sales order? I have created several other sales orders for this sales organization, where I used the same profit center and that caused no problems.

Kind regards,

Floyd

entry.title: Multiple tax calculation on a Single Line of Order/billing
entry.content:
Dear All,

I have a business situation that requests me to use handling and freight as condition types in the SD pricing procedure. We are adding freight and handling as condition type on the first line of the order/billing line item.

I also have a business requirement to calculate tax on freight and handling separately from the product price in line 1 of the order/billing.

As per standard SAP, only one tax code/call is allowed per line. How can I calculate different taxes on product vs Freight assuming both product price and freight charges are on line 1 of the order/billing?

Please note that we are using Vertex for tax calculation.

Thanks

Avnish

entry.title: Duplicate spool is getting generated in MIGO for Mtyp 311.
entry.content:
Hello Friends

When i am trying to do transfer posting in MIGO, movement type 311 for custom Output , duplicate spools are getting generated which is causing paper wastage.

Replication steps.Step 1. Run the tcode MIGO
Step 2. Choose A08 Transfer posting, R10 Other
Step 3. Enter material <XXXXXXXXXX>, Plant XXXX, Issuing Storage location XXXX, Destination storage location XXXX and Quantity 1.
Step 4. Click on Post.

Did debugging there is no code to generate duplicate spools.

And even choosing Individual slip while posting.

 

Regards

Naeeum

entry.title: Overlapping fields in the pop-up associated with the occasional customer when creating a sales order
entry.content:
Hi

I'm having a problem with the occasional customer, i.e. when I put the sold-to party “sporadic” in the sales order, when I open the pop-up to change the data associated with the customer at the time of the sale, overlapping fields appear, in particular the field relating to the tax number.

 

As you can see in the image, the other attributes tab and its fields appear overlapping, making it impossible to fill in those fields.

I don't know if this error has to do with the system version, as it's S/4HANA, version 102.

Has anyone had this problem?

Thanks
